name: 15-truncated-stream
description: Canonical scenario #15: truncated stream (TCP close mid-event with no terminal marker). Distinct from #13's mid-stream 5xx: the emulator delivers a few well-formed events, writes the head of one more event (without the trailing `\n\n` separator and no `data: [DONE]` / `message_stop`), then calls socket.destroy() to forcibly close the TCP connection. The SDK's HTTP client must surface this as a 'connection closed mid-stream' / 'unexpected end of stream' typed error — distinct path from receiving a typed error chunk because there's no error chunk to receive, just a vanished socket. Ambiguity call: at the emulator layer, this is genuinely different from #13 (no error event written) but both #13 and #15 end with res.end() vs. res.socket.destroy() respectively; the SDK-observable difference is whether bytes for an error event arrive before the close. The comparator config matches #13/#14 since the SDK-level surfacing of 'stream ended unexpectedly' varies between the two SDKs in the same structural ways.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: TLS encrypts data in transit using asymmetric handshakes.
TLS encrypts data in transit using asymmetric handshakes.
